title: Participant-Relative Admissible Transport Geometry
canonical_statement: Traversability is the geometry of usable continuity.

formal_statement.statement: Traversability emerges only where transport continuity remains sufficiently accessible, navigable, coherent, and recursively admissible relative to participating agents under recursive constraint dynamics.
formal_statement.core_distinction: transport existence does not imply traversability
formal_statement.participant_relative_condition: transport becomes traversable only when it is usable for recursively preservable participation

participant_relative_admissible_transpor.interpretation: \mathfrak T_{\mathrm{adm}} denotes the participant-relative admissible subgeometry of transport continuity accessible to recursively participating agents.

usable_continuity_geometry.interpretation: Traversability selects the usable subset of continuity structure through which recursive participation remains sustainably possible.
negative_conditions: not all connected structure is participatorily accessible

traversability_admissibility_condition.interpretation: Traversability requires both transport continuity and participant-relative admissible access to that continuity.

participatory_accessibility_principle.statement: Traversability is not merely path existence.
participatory_accessibility_principle.compression: Traversability transforms transport into usable participation.
participatory_accessibility_principle.interpretation: Traversability converts potential movement into admissible future participation.

recursive_viability_consequence.interpretation: Viability preserves only sufficiently traversable continuity structure capable of sustaining recursively recoverable participation.

wave_topological_connection.interpretation: Participatory traversability depends on recoverable future-fiber topology, continuity-wave coherence, and participant-relative admissible transport geometry.

primitive_stack_position.interpretation: Transport opens possible movement, but traversability selects usable continuity for participant-relative future reachability.

collapse_geometry.interpretation: Collapse may occur when transport remains present but no longer supports admissible participant-relative traversability.

recovery_geometry.interpretation: Recovery restores usable continuity by reopening participant-relative admissible transport pathways.

canonical_compression: Traversability is the geometry of usable continuity.
Traversability organizes through participant-relative admissible transport geometry.
Traversability transforms transport into usable participation.
deep_compression_forms: Transport alone is insufficient for traversability.
Continuity must be usable to support recursive participation.
Admissible transport geometry selects usable continuity pathways.
Viability preserves traversable continuity, not mere continuity.
Collapse may preserve transport while losing usable participation.
one_sentence_lock: Traversability is participant-relative usable continuity: transport becomes viable only when it remains admissibly navigable for recursively preservable participation.
